headlines.title: United Arab Emirates quits OPEC as Iran war raises Gulf tensions
headlines.description: U.S. crude oil surpassed $100 per barrel for the first time since April 10 after Iran talks stumbled and the United Arab Emirates said it would leave OPEC.

headlines.title: U.S. appears cool on Iran proposal to end war and reopen Hormuz without a nuclear deal
headlines.description: The United States showed little immediate enthusiasm for a new Iranian proposal that would end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz without resolving the impasse over the Islamic Republic's nuclear program.

headlines.title: Graphic: Tracking ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has been at a near-standstill for weeks amid the Iran war, sending prices of oil and other key goods soaring.

headlines.title: How the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner chaos unfolded
headlines.description: Secret Service faced challenges evacuating President, Vice President, Cabinet officials and Congressional leaders after gunman rushes D.C. gala.

headlines.title: FCC to direct Disney-owned TV stations to file early license renewals, source says
headlines.description: The Federal Communications Commission is expected to issue an order Tuesday directing Disney’s eight owned-and-operated television stations to file their broadcast license renewals ahead of schedule, according to a source with knowledge of the matter

headlines.title: White House condemns 'political violence' after correspondents' dinner shooting
headlines.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt began a press briefing by thanking the law enforcement response to the White House Correspondents' Association dinner and condemning "political violence" and rising rhetoric in the country.

headlines.title: Suspect Cole Tomas Allen charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump
headlines.description: The man accused of opening fire outside the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner was charged with three counts in his first court appearance, including attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump. Cole Tomas Allen also faces charges of interstate transportation of weapons and discharge of a firearm during a violent crime.

headlines.title: Trump welcomes King Charles: 'Wounds of war healed into the most cherished friendship'
headlines.description: President Trump spoke at a White House ceremony for King Charles and Queen Camilla. The president praised the "noble spirit of the British" and recalled the historic partnership between the nations.

headlines.title: Former NBA player and coach Damon Jones pleads guilty to selling gamblers inside information
headlines.description: Former NBA player and assistant coach Damon Jones pleaded guilty on Tuesday to federal charges that he gave inside information to gamblers and took part in rigged poker games.

headlines.title: Federal agents conducting raids connected to investigation into alleged welfare fraud in Minnesota
headlines.description: Federal law enforcement agencies are conducting a series of raids connected to the investigation into alleged welfare fraud in Minnesota on Tuesday morning, federal authorities have said.

headlines.title: Taylor Swift Files to Trademark Voice and Image to Protect From AI
headlines.description: Taylor Swift is proactively moving to ensure her voice and likeness are protected from deepfakes and other AI misuse. Swift’s company filed three applications last week, including two audio trademarks for “Hey, it’s Taylor Swift” and another for “Hey, it’s Taylor,” as well as a third filing for a visual trademark covering a photo of Swift holding a pink guitar. NBC’s Liz Kreutz reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Trump marks U.S. independence in U.K. welcome speech
headlines.description: While welcoming King Charles to the White House, President Trump said it might seem "ironic" that the U.S. is honoring the British king as the country marks the 250th anniversary of American independence. "But in fact, no tribute could be more appropriate," he said.
